title: harpalyce brasiliana
intro: Harpalyce brasiliana, also known as the Brazil clover or butterfly pea, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, native to Brazil and other parts of South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing various health benefits, including cardiovascular protection and anti-cancer properties. Harpalyce brasiliana can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine.
